calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Luis Duenas, Esq. (818.534.3102 or 818.534.3104) of Persolve Legal Group LLP for Judgment Creditor via Zoom (audio only).
Carnell Freeman (415.260.6843; carnell.freeman@gmail.com) Judgment Debtor, in propria persona.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CROWN ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is taken under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

***Order signed after hearing***
Defendant Carnell Freeman's claim of exemption is granted in part. Defendant improperly included disposable earnings as required withholdings. The Sheriff is ordered to release the $209.21 as well as any other funds he/she is holding to the judgment creditor. Defendant shall pay $200.00 per biweekly pay period to service the debt.

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Apr-25-2022 Continued To Apr-26-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Carnell Freeman (415.260.6843; carnell.freeman@gmail.com) in propria persona.
No other appearances.

The Court noted there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule and rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CROWN ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is continued to April 26, 2022 at 9:30am.
The clerk notified counsel for Judgment Creditor.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Feb-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LUIS DUENAS, Esq. of Persolve Legal Group, LLP (Tel: 818-534-3100) for plaintiff/judgment creditor Crown Asset Management, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

PLAINTIFF/JUDGMENT CREDICROWN ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's claim of exemption is granted in part. Defendant improperly includes disposable earnings as required withholdings. Defendant also failed to list his insurance payments as a monthly expense. The Sheriff is ordered to release the $1,451.49 as well as any other funds he/she is holding to the judgment creditor. Defendant shall pay $600 every two weeks to service the debt.

Order signed. Copies of signed order mailed to Sacramento County Sheriff's Office and the parties.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
